subject: Mathematics
options:
only (I) is TRUE

only (II) is TRUE

both (I) and (II) are TRUE

neither (I) nor (II) is TRUE
solution:
For

$$ f:[1,\infty)\to [1,\infty),\qquad f(x)=(x-1)^4+1, $$

we first find the inverse function.

Since for $x\ge 1$, the function $(x-1)^4+1$ is strictly increasing, $f$ is one-one and onto from $[1,\infty)$ to $[1,\infty)$.

Let

$$ y=(x-1)^4+1. $$

Then

$$ y-1=(x-1)^4. $$

Since $x\ge 1$, we take the positive fourth root:

$$ x-1=(y-1)^{1/4}. $$

Hence

$$ f^{-1}(y)=1+(y-1)^{1/4}. $$

So

$$ f^{-1}(x)=1+(x-1)^{1/4}. $$

Now check the two statements one by one.

Statement (I)

We need to solve

$$ f(x)=f^{-1}(x). $$

That is,

$$ (x-1)^4+1=1+(x-1)^{1/4}. $$

So

$$ (x-1)^4=(x-1)^{1/4}. $$

Put

$$ t=x-1 \quad \text{where } t\ge 0. $$

Then the equation becomes

$$ t^4=t^{1/4}. $$

Now solve:

$$ t^4-t^{1/4}=0. $$

This gives

$$ t^{1/4}\left(t^{15/4}-1\right)=0. $$

Hence

$$ t=0 \quad \text{or} \quad t^{15/4}=1. $$

Since $t\ge 0$, from $t^{15/4}=1$ we get

$$ t=1. $$

Therefore

$$ x-1=0 \Rightarrow x=1, $$

or

$$ x-1=1 \Rightarrow x=2. $$

So the set

$$ S=\{x\in[1,\infty): f(x)=f^{-1}(x)\} $$

contains exactly two elements:

$$ S=\{1,2\}. $$

Thus, Statement (I) is TRUE.

Statement (II)

We need to solve

$$ f(x)=f^{-1}(x+1). $$

Now

$$ f(x)=(x-1)^4+1, $$

and

$$ f^{-1}(x+1)=1+\big((x+1)-1\big)^{1/4}=1+x^{1/4}. $$

So the equation becomes

$$ (x-1)^4+1=1+x^{1/4}. $$

Hence

$$ (x-1)^4=x^{1/4}. $$

Again put

$$ t=x^{1/4}, \quad t\ge 1 \text{ since } x\ge 1. $$

Then

$$ x=t^4. $$

So the equation becomes

$$ (t^4-1)^4=t. $$

Instead of solving this directly, test simple values.

For $x=1$:

$$ f(1)=(1-1)^4+1=1, $$

and

$$ f^{-1}(1+1)=f^{-1}(2)=1+(2-1)^{1/4}=2. $$

So $x=1$ is not a solution.

For $x=2$:

$$ f(2)=(2-1)^4+1=2, $$

and

$$ f^{-1}(2+1)=f^{-1}(3)=1+(3-1)^{1/4}=1+2^{1/4}\neq 2. $$

So $x=2$ is not a solution.

Now define

$$ g(x)=(x-1)^4-x^{1/4}, \qquad x\ge 1. $$

We need to check whether $g(x)=0$ has any solution.

Observe:

$$ g(1)=0-1=-1<0, $$

and

$$ g(2)=1-2^{1/4}<0, $$

while

$$ g(3)=16-3^{1/4}>0. $$

Since $g$ is continuous, there is at least one root in $(2,3)$.

So the set

$$ S=\{x\in[1,\infty): f(x)=f^{-1}(x+1)\} $$

is not empty.

Thus, Statement (II) is FALSE.

Therefore, the correct option is

$$ \boxed{\text{Option A: only (I) is TRUE}} $$

subject: Mathematics
solution:
$$y = {5^{\log x}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \log y = \log x.log5$$

$$ \Rightarrow \log x = {{\log y} \over {\log 5}} = {\log _5}y$$

$$ \Rightarrow x = {e^{{{\log }_5}y}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow x = {y^{{{\log }_5}e}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow x = {y^{{1 \over {\log 5}}}}$$
title: JEE Main 2021 (Online) 17th March Morning Shift

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Finding inverse of f(x)

$$y = {{x - 2} \over {x - 3}} \Rightarrow xy - 3y = x - 2 \Rightarrow x(y - 1) = 3y - 2$$

$$ \therefore $$ $${f^{ - 1}}(x) = {{3x - 2} \over {x - 1}}$$

Similarly for $${g^{ - 1}}(x)$$

$$y = 2x - 3 \Rightarrow x = {{y + 3} \over 2} \Rightarrow {g^{ - 1}}(x) = {{x + 3} \over 2}$$

$$ \therefore $$ $${{3x - 2} \over {x - 1}} + {{x + 3} \over 2} = {{13} \over 2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 6x - 4 + {x^2} + 2x - 3 = 13x - 13$$

$$ \Rightarrow {x^2} - 5x + 6 = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow (x - 2)(x - 3) = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ x = 2 or 3

subject: Mathematics
solution:
f(x) = $${{{8^{2x}} - {8^{ - 2x}}} \over {{8^{2x}} + {8^{ - 2x}}}}$$ = y

$$ \therefore $$ $${{y + 1} \over {y - 1}} = {{{{2.8}^{2x}}} \over { - {{2.8}^{ - 2x}}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $${{1 + y} \over {1 - y}}$$ = 84x

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $${\log _e}\left( {{{1 + y} \over {1 - y}}} \right)$$ = 4x $${\log _e}8$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ x = $${1 \over {4{{\log }_e}8}}{\log _e}\left( {{{1 + y} \over {1 - y}}} \right)$$

$$ \therefore $$ f-1(x) = $${1 \over 4}\left( {{{\log }_8}e} \right){\log _e}\left( {{{1 + x} \over {1 - x}}} \right)$$
title: JEE Main 2020 (Online) 8th January Morning Slot

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Assume,

y = f(x)

$$ \Rightarrow $$ y = $${{x - 1} \over {x - 2}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ yx - 2y = x - 1

$$ \Rightarrow $$ (y - 1)x = 2y - 1

$$ \Rightarrow $$ x = $${{2y - 1} \over {y - 1}}$$ = f -1(y)

As on the given domain the function is invertible and its inverse can be computed as shown above.
title: JEE Main 2018 (Online) 15th April Evening Slot

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Here, $$f(x) = {{b - x} \over {1 - bx}}$$

where, 0 < b < 1, 0 < x < 1

For function to be invertible it should be one-one onto.

$$\therefore$$ Check range :

Let $$f(x) = y \Rightarrow y = {{b - x} \over {1 - bx}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow y - bxy = b - x \Rightarrow x(1 - by) = b - y$$

$$ \Rightarrow x = {{b - y} \over {1 - by}}$$

where, 0 < x < 1

$$\therefore$$ $$0 < {{b - y} \over {1 - by}} < 1$$

$${{b - y} \over {1 - by}} > 0$$ and $${{b - y} \over {1 - by}} < 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow y < b$$ or $$y > {1 \over b}$$ .... (i)

$${{(b - 1)(y + 1)} \over {1 - by}} < - 1 < y < {1 \over b}$$ ..... (ii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$$y \in \left( { - 1,{1 \over b}} \right) \subset $$ Codomain

Thus, f(x) is not invertible.
title: IIT-JEE 2011 Paper 2 Offline
